heading: Avoid Workloads on the Destination Cluster
body:
Writes are blocked during the live migration:

- When a live migration starts, writes to the destination cluster
  are blocked. Attempts to perform writes to the destination cluster
  fail.

- Before proceeding with the cutover, you must manually stop writes to
  your source cluster. Atlas enforces this by enabling write blocking
  on the source cluster during the cutover process.

  > **Important:** Source and destination cluster write blocking during cutover occurs if
  > you are migrating source cluster running MongoDB version 6.0 and later.

- After a live migration completes successfully or fails, writes to the
  destination cluster may continue.

  > **Note:** Atlas automatically attempts to remove the write blocks when the
  > migration is complete.
  >
  > If you need to manually remove the write block on your cluster, use
  > the [setUserWriteBlockMode](manual/reference/command/setUserWriteBlockMode) command with the `global` field
  > set to `false`:
  >
  > ```sh
  > db.adminCommand( {
  >   setUserWriteBlockMode: 1,
  >   global: false
  > } )
  > ```
  >
  > To run the `setUserWriteBlockMode` command, the user must
  > have the [SET\_USER\_WRITE\_BLOCK](manual/reference/privilege-actions) privilege.

Don't run multiple migrations to the same destination cluster at the
same time.

Don't start the cutover process for your applications to the destination
cluster while the live migration process is syncing.
